hero.title: Project Tracker
hero.description: Organize projects, tasks, priorities, due dates, and progress signals inside the Ansiversa shell with user-owned database persistence.

flow.title: Projects → Tasks → Insights
steps: Create project records with owner, status, priority, due date, and notes
Add tasks to projects with status, priority, estimates, due dates, and notes
Review progress, blocked work, overdue tasks, and upcoming due dates

differences.title: Keep project tracking focused and private
differences.description: Project Tracker gives users a lightweight planning workspace without turning Ansiversa into a full enterprise project management platform.

items.title: Plan
items.description: Create project records with only the fields needed for personal or small-team review.

items.title: Track
items.description: Add and update tasks with practical status, priority, estimate, and due date signals.

items.title: Review
items.description: Use saved insights to see active work, blocked tasks, overdue items, and upcoming deadlines.

feel.title: A calm workspace for active work
feel.description: Project Tracker should feel direct, compact, and operational rather than like a heavy project-management suite.

items.title: Easy to revisit
items.description: Projects and tasks persist as user-owned records for quick daily review.

items.title: Focused by design
items.description: V1 avoids teams, permissions, comments, files, chat, and integrations.

items.title: Protected by default
items.description: Project and task records are scoped to the signed-in user.

audiences.title: Who uses Project Tracker
audiences.description: Project Tracker is useful for Ansiversa users who need a small, private planning board inside the platform.

items.title: Solo builders
items.description: Track personal projects, side work, or launch checklists.

items.title: Students and professionals
items.description: Organize assignments, work streams, deadlines, and next actions.

items.title: Families and small teams
items.description: Keep simple project notes and tasks together without adopting a larger tool.

resourceSection.title: Project Tracker essentials
resourceSection.description: The V1 workflow prioritizes lightweight project records, task lists, and review signals.

items.title: Projects
items.description: Create, edit, delete, search, and filter saved project records.

items.title: Tasks
items.description: Create, edit, delete, search, and filter project tasks.

items.title: Insights
items.description: Review active projects, completed projects, blocked tasks, overdue tasks, and upcoming tasks.

groupedSection.title: A compact project review loop
groupedSection.description: Project Tracker starts with the smallest useful loop for repeat planning.

items.name: Project record
items.description: Capture title, owner, status, priority, due date, and notes.

items.name: Task record
items.description: Attach tasks to projects with status, priority, estimate, due date, and notes.

items.name: Filters
items.description: Find projects and tasks by text, project, or status.

items.name: Insights
items.description: See saved totals, blocked work, overdue tasks, and upcoming due dates.

technicalSection.title: Aligned with the Ansiversa platform model
technicalSection.description: Project Tracker runs inside the shared shell with isolated backend ownership for V1 project data.
items: API-driven overview metadata with CTA routing to /project-tracker/projects.
Catalog key, slug, logo key, and route identity remain project-tracker.
Protected workflow pages use shared authenticated page state.
Backend owns isolated ProjectTrackerProjects and ProjectTrackerTasks tables with owner-scoped APIs.

finalCta.title: Open Project Tracker
finalCta.description: Create a project and start organizing tasks from the protected workspace.
